name: Wholemeal Red and Yellow Pepper Quiche with P
recipeIngredient: Butter, red and yellow pepper filling:
Red pepper, seeded & thinly sliced, half
Yellow pepper, seeded & thinly sliced, half
Cheddar or Gruyere cheese, shredded, 2 oz (50 g)
recipeInstructions:
- The Latin name for pansies is Viola x wittrockiana.
- This colorful starter makes a great supper or lunch dish served with fresh peas or a leafy salad. Its bright colors make it appealing to both adults and children, and it's easy to make.
1. Make the flan case: Sift whole wheat flour and salt into a large bowl. Add margarine and mix until the mixture resembles fine breadcrumbs. Lift your fingers above the bowl as you rub to keep the mixture light or aerated. Add ice water and mix with a knife until you have a firm dough. Wrap in cling film and chill for 5-10 minutes.
2. Preheat oven to 350°F (180°C) / gas mark 4.
3. On a lightly floured surface, roll out the dough into a circle large enough to line an 8-inch/20-24 cm flan dish. Grease the dish and line it with the dough. Set aside.
4. To make the filling: Heat butter and oil in a shallow pan. Add onions and peppers and sweat (cook) gently until vegetables are soft but not colored. Add pansies. Whisk eggs, stir in cheese and milk, then season to taste.
5. Pour onion and pepper mixture into egg mixture and stir well. Pour into prepared flan case.
6. Bake for 25-30 minutes, or until filling is nicely puffed up and pastry begins to brown. Serve hot or cold.
